key_highlights: U.S. added 172,000 jobs in May, unemployment steady at 4.3%, but hot labor data pushes Fed rate-hike odds higher, with ECB already hiking as G7 lead hawk.
Tech sell-off deepens: chip stocks lose over $1 trillion in market cap, Nasdaq and S&P 500 suffer worst day of year, driven by AI valuation fears and rising rate expectations.
Geopolitical tensions escalate: Iran war nears 100 days, Asia-to-US container rates spike 109%, and Trump holds profanity-filled call with Netanyahu.
Bitcoin falls below $60,000 for first time since 2024 Trump win, as hedge funds dump 31,400 BTC in Q1 while banks quietly double Bitcoin ETF positions.
Google signs $1 billion/month cloud deal with SpaceX, while Nvidia pivots to selling AI factories instead of chips, and Wedbush raises PT on Palo Alto Networks.
Consumer pressures mount: mortgage rates rise, gas dips 17 cents but inflation squeezes, and best CD/savings rates hover around 4% APY.
Market breadth weakens: Wall Street's hottest trade cracks in trillion-dollar wipeout, while dividend ETFs like the smartest one up 19% in 2026 offer refuge.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off with over $1 trillion in market cap erased; Nvidia, AMD, Micron, and other AI beneficiaries hit hard. Nvidia pivots to AI factories, but valuation concerns persist. CrowdStrike and Datadog among best performers in May, but broad tech weakness dominates.
sectors.implications: Investors are rotating out of high-multiple AI names amid rate-hike fears and profit-taking. Long-term AI demand remains intact, but near-term volatility is extreme. Opportunities in cybersecurity (PANW, CRWD) and cloud (DDOG) may offer relative strength.

sectors.trend: Iran war drives container rates up 109% Asia-to-US, jet fuel costs rising, but U.S. gas prices dip 17 cents nationally. Raizen inks $13 billion out-of-court deal with creditors, indicating stress in biofuel/energy sector.
sectors.implications: Supply chain disruptions and higher shipping costs will feed into inflation, complicating Fed policy. Energy stocks may benefit from geopolitical premium, but consumer discretionary spending could suffer.

sectors.trend: Fed's Barr warns of risks from looser Wall Street bank rules. Mortgage rates rising, HELOC rates expected to increase. Hedge funds dump Bitcoin, but banks quietly add Bitcoin ETF exposure. Prediction markets gaining traction (Susquehanna).
sectors.implications: Banking sector faces regulatory uncertainty and rate risk. Higher rates benefit net interest margins but could slow loan demand. Bitcoin ETF flows show institutional divergence.

sectors.trend: Costco reiterated as Buy by Morgan Stanley, Coca-Cola explores India listing. Consumer squeezed by inflation, but premium travel appetite remains strong (Delta). Mohawk Industries adapting to market pressures, Leggett & Platt weak.
sectors.implications: Defensive consumer staples and discount retailers may outperform. Luxury (LVMH fall from grace) and discretionary housing-related stocks face headwinds.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates rising, home equity loan rates expected to increase. Weyerhaeuser, Louisiana-Pacific, Boise Cascade report mixed Q1 results. Frasers weighs £500 million bid for shopping center in UK.
sectors.implications: Higher rates pressure housing affordability and REITs. However, some timber/wood products companies may benefit from supply constraints. Commercial real estate remains bifurcated.

sectors.trend: JetBlue CEO speaks on potential consolidation at IATA. Delta President discusses Middle East flights and premium travel. IATA director warns of air transport stagflation and rising jet fuel costs.
sectors.implications: Airlines face cost headwinds from fuel and geopolitical disruptions. Consolidation may improve pricing power but regulatory hurdles remain. Premium travel demand is a bright spot.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk premium (Iran war, supply chains)
Fed/ECB rate-hike expectations and inflation persistence
Infrastructure and defense spending (SpaceX-Google deal, nuclear reactor test)

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed rate hike (or hawkish pause) due to hot jobs data and sticky inflation
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration risk, favor floating-rate bonds, increase cash and short-term Treasuries. Consider defensive sectors (utilities, healthcare, consumer staples).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation (Iran war, US-Cuba sanctions, Bolivia unrest)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy, defense, and commodities. Hedge via gold or inflation-protected securities. Avoid regions directly affected.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech/AI bubble deflation and valuation compression
risk_assessment.mitigation: Trim overvalued AI names, take profits in high-beta tech. Rotate to quality tech with strong cash flows (e.g., MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL). Use stop-losses.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Supply chain disruption from Iran war and container rate spikes
risk_assessment.mitigation: Invest in logistics, shipping, and domestic manufacturing. Avoid companies heavily reliant on Asia-to-US supply chains. Monitor inventory levels.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Crypto market volatility and regulatory uncertainty
risk_assessment.mitigation: Limit crypto exposure to small allocation. Focus on Bitcoin ETF flows as institutional signal. Avoid altcoins with unclear regulatory status.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer credit and housing stress from rising rates
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid highly leveraged consumer and homebuilder stocks. Favor companies with pricing power and low debt. Monitor mortgage delinquency data.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate quality cybersecurity stocks (PANW, CRWD) on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Wedbush raised PT on PANW, CrowdStrike reported strong FQ1 2027 earnings. Cybersecurity spending remains resilient regardless of rate environment.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy dividend ETFs or high-quality dividend stocks (e.g., COST, MSFT, AAPL)
opportunities.rationale: Smartest dividend ETF up 19% in 2026. Defensive income plays offer stability amid tech sell-off. Costco reiterated Buy by Morgan Stanley, MSFT and AAPL are big company buys.

opportunities.recommendation: Add exposure to energy and defense via ETFs or select stocks
opportunities.rationale: Geopolitical tensions (Iran war, US-Cuba) and rising jet fuel costs benefit energy. Defense spending likely to increase. Google-SpaceX deal highlights space/defense tech.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider small modular nuclear reactor plays (SMR)
opportunities.rationale: First test reached criticality, signaling a new energy frontier. Long-term play on clean baseload power for AI data centers.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation and short-term Treasuries (4%+ yields)
defensive_moves.rationale: Best CD rates at 4% APY, money market at 4.01%, high-yield savings at 4.1%. Cash provides optionality amid market volatility and rate uncertainty.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put options or inverse ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Tech sell-off and 'trillion-dollar wipeout' suggest further downside. Use QQQ puts or SH for short-term protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-beta AI and semiconductor stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Nvidia, AMD, Micron face valuation compression and profit-taking. Bank of America reset Nvidia forecast, chip selloff erased $1 trillion. Rotate to value/defensive.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid consumer discretionary and housing-related stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising mortgage rates, weak Q1 for Leggett & Platt, Mohawk adapting to pressures. Consumer squeezed by inflation. Favor staples over discretionary.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish bias with elevated volatility. The tech sell-off may extend as rate-hike fears persist and geopolitical risks (Iran war, supply chains) weigh. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq could test key support levels (Nifty 23,000 in India, S&P 500 4,500). Defensive rotation into dividends, cash, and energy likely. Bitcoin may remain under pressure below $60k.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic. AI and cloud spending remain secular growth drivers (Google-SpaceX, Nvidia AI factories). Fed likely to pause or cut once inflation moderates. Earnings resilience in quality names (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, COST) should support a recovery. Geopolitical resolution could unlock upside. Dividend and value stocks may outperform growth.
key_catalysts: Iran war developments and potential ceasefire
ECB rate decision and forward guidance
Nvidia GTC or AI conference announcements
U.S. CPI and PCE inflation data (May/June)
World Cup 2026 preparations and consumer spending
China regulatory changes on programme trading
Crypto regulatory clarity and Bitcoin ETF flows
watch_list: Bitcoin price and ETF net flows
WTI crude oil price (above $90/bbl adds inflation pressure)
Tech sector earnings revisions (especially NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL)
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
